channel.title: Osteoperionews
channel.description: Curated news from the periodontal and implant literature — Dr. Ernesto Bruschi

item.title: At 20 years implants hold up like natural teeth — but only with maintenance
item.description: In brief: Over 20 years, implants and contra-lateral teeth showed comparable attachment loss (~4.5 vs ~4.4 mm) and survival in maintained periodontitis…

item.title: Antibiotics add half a millimetre in peri-implantitis — local beats systemic
item.description: In brief: Across 22 RCTs, adjunctive antibiotics improved PPD by ~0.69 mm and CAL by ~0.55 mm over debridement alone — statistically significant,…

item.title: Xenogeneic blocks match autogenous bone at 4 years — except in the esthetic zone
item.description: In brief: At 4 years, collagenated xenogeneic and autogenous bone blocks gave comparable implant survival and success after lateral ridge augmentation.

item.title: Fascia lata allograft thickens peri-implant mucosa without a palatal harvest
item.description: In brief: In a 16-patient pilot RCT, fascia lata allograft and connective tissue graft both significantly increased peri-implant mucosal thickness and…

item.title: Patients on Ozempic-class drugs: a risk modifier in perio, not a treatment
item.description: In brief: GLP-1 receptor agonists may affect periodontal tissues both indirectly (glycemic control, weight, inflammation) and directly (GLP-1R / DPP-4…

item.title: Past 10 cigarettes a day, GBR implants lose twice the bone
item.description: In brief: In this 5-year cohort, smokers of >10 cigarettes/day lost about twice the marginal bone (2.67 mm) of non- and light smokers (1.34-1.40 mm).

item.title: PEEK or titanium mesh for the ridge? They tie once you control the graft
item.description: In brief: RCT in 14 patients (28 sites): no significant difference in horizontal bone gain between prebent titanium mesh (3.02 mm) and customized PEEK…

item.title: Thin-phenotype lower recessions: the tunnel beats the envelope flap
item.description: In brief: RCT in 59 patients (103 lower-incisor sites, thin phenotype): at 6 months the tunnel + CTG gave 79% mean root coverage vs 58% for the envelope…

item.title: Putting a number on periodontitis as cardiovascular risk: a proposed score
item.description: In brief: A narrative review proposing the Endothelial Risk Score (ERS): it combines Periodontal Inflamed Surface Area (PISA) with age, sex, systolic BP,…
item.category: American heart journal plus : cardiology research and practice

item.title: Oral disease leads NCD prevalence — and periodontal disability rises with wealth
item.description: In brief: In 2023, oral disorders in Latin America and the Caribbean caused 308 million prevalent cases and 2.41 million years lived with disability —…

item.title: GBR membranes: a 26% complication rate forces a rethink beyond passive occlusion
item.description: In brief: A meta-analysis of 100 studies puts GBR complication rates near 26%, exposing the structural limits of conventional collagen and PTFE membranes.

item.title: Augmented vs native posterior mandible: which bone hosts healthier implants at 15 years?
item.description: In brief: Implant survival was nearly identical at 15 years: 94.7% in native bone, 95.3% in augmented bone.
item.category: International Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

item.title: Peri-implantitis debridement: less than 1 mm cleaned in this SEM cohort
item.description: In brief: SEM analysis of 49 explanted implants found a mean contaminant-free zone of just 0.66 mm (range 0–3.0 mm).

item.title: Perio therapy as CVD prevention: cost-effective only over a lifetime horizon
item.description: In brief: Over a 25-year horizon, NSPT reached an ICER of €19,322/QALY — within NICE thresholds; at 10 years the ICER more than doubled to €49,847.

item.title: EMD at 30: where the evidence holds — and where it does not
item.description: In brief: EMD remains a validated adjunct for recession coverage and intrabony defects, with consistent evidence across 30 years of literature.

item.title: Full-arch implants: what 29 Italian experts agreed on — and what it changes in practice
item.description: In brief: Four implants in the mandibular full-arch show comparable survival to six, per the consensus statements.

item.title: Regeneration then orthodontics: 20-year tooth survival in Stage IV periodontitis
item.description: In brief: In this cohort, 89.1% estimated tooth survival at 20 years for severely compromised teeth (PPD ≥7 mm, intrabony defect, pathologic migration)…

item.title: Short implants at 10 years: viable for the atrophic ridge, but survival stays below standard implants
item.description: In brief: Pooled 10-year survival was 91–94% (patient/implant level); sensitivity analysis returned the more conservative 89.7–92.8%.

item.title: Simultaneous GBR with a 3D preformed PLGA membrane: 87% bone gain maintained at 5 months, zero exposures
item.description: In brief: In this 21-site single-arm study, mean horizontal bone augmentation was 2.99 mm with 87.2% of hard tissue gain preserved at 5 months.

item.title: Connective Tissue Graft Under Pontics: Early Gain, Uncertain Long-Term Edge
item.description: In brief: CTG reliably improves ridge contour and soft-tissue volume at pontic sites in the short term.

item.title: Full-arch implant prostheses: the implants survive, but material choice drives complication risk
item.description: In brief: Prosthesis survival was 92.3% and implant loss 4.2% over a mean 6.8 years — high reliability in this cohort.

item.title: Periodontal disease starts in childhood — but Asia's data can't yet prove how often
item.description: In brief: Gingivitis was the dominant finding across 84 studies from 24 Asian countries, with prevalence spanning under 10% to 100% — a range that…

item.title: Socket Shield: FEA points to 120-150° arc as the biomechanical sweet spot for the retained fragment
item.description: In brief: In this finite element model, a labial arc angle of 120-150° for the retained root fragment produced the most balanced stress distribution…

item.title: Zirconia vs titanium for overdentures: a 14-point success gap and more bone loss at 3 years
item.description: In brief: In this 3-year split-mouth RCT, titanium implant success (91.7%) outpaced zirconia (77.8%) by nearly 14 percentage points.

item.title: Autonomous robotics for transcrestal sinus elevation: submillimetre trueness in a first cohort
item.description: In brief: In this 12-implant cohort, autonomous robotic placement achieved mean global deviations of 0.58 mm (coronal) and 0.64 mm (apical), with 1.19°…

item.title: SRP alone beats the adjunct: NaOCl/AA gel adds no microbiological benefit in this RCT
item.description: In brief: A single 30-second NaOCl/AA gel application during SRP produced no additional microbiological benefit over mechanical debridement alone.

item.title: PRF and platelet concentrates in bone regeneration: adjunct, not replacement — what the evidence actually supports
item.description: In brief: L-PRF and A-PRF show clinically relevant adjunctive benefit in periodontal intrabony defects, correlating with improved CAL gain and faster soft…

item.title: Free gingival graft donor site: CTA alone shortens closure time and reduces mid-healing pain
item.description: In brief: In this 60-patient RCT, α-cyanoacrylate adhesive alone shortened intraoperative wound closure time significantly vs both suture-based groups…

item.title: Plasma-activated healing abutments: a histological signal at the epithelial interface
item.description: In brief: Plasma activation of healing abutments produced lower inflammation scores in the basal zone only (P = 0.04); upper and intermediate zones showed…

item.title: Implants at 20 years: a 93% survival rate — but half of failures are mechanical, not biological
item.description: In brief: Pooled 20-year survival was 93.0% (CI 91.6–94.1%) across 1,028 implants in prospective studies — a reassuring baseline figure for patient…
item.category: International journal of oral and maxillofacial surgery

item.title: Implants in diabetes: HbA1c below 7% is the threshold, not the diagnosis
item.description: In brief: Well-controlled diabetics (HbA1c ≤7%) showed outcomes comparable to non-diabetic patients in this review.

item.title: Mandibular bone repair: the periosteal stem-cell switch that could replace grafts
item.description: In brief: The PLAGL1-KLF4-IRX5 transcription factor axis controls osteoblast differentiation of periosteal stem/progenitor cells in the mandible.

item.title: Gut dysbiosis as a causal driver of periodontal bone loss: FMT evidence in a mouse model
item.description: In brief: In a combined UC + periodontitis mouse model, gut dysbiosis causally doubled alveolar bone loss via fecal microbiota transplantation.

item.title: Dentine vs e-PRF for GBR: equivalent outcomes, your choice
item.description: In brief: In this 40-patient RCT, DDBM and e-PRF produced identical implant survival (100%) and marginal bone loss (~1.2–1.3 mm buccal) at 24 months.

item.title: Tissue-level implants at 25 years: prosthesis design doesn't drive bone loss — suppuration does
item.description: In brief: After 25 years, crestal bone loss and peri-implantitis prevalence did not differ significantly between single crowns, splinted crowns, or…
item.category: Clinical implant dentistry and related research

item.title: ISQ at placement: useful stability indicator, but not a predictor of long-term survival
item.description: In brief: ISQ shows a moderate, statistically significant correlation with insertion torque (pooled r = 0.44) across 20 pooled studies, but shared…
item.category: Clinical implant dentistry and related research

item.title: Attached gingiva width: not an independent bone-loss driver in this 5-year cohort
item.description: In brief: In univariate analysis, implants with <2 mm attached gingiva showed greater mean bone loss (2.17 vs 1.64 mm), but this difference did not…

item.title: FGG vs. collagen matrix for peri-implant keratinized tissue: autograft wins at 6 months
item.description: In brief: In this RCT, FGG produced significantly greater keratinized tissue width and soft tissue thickness than XCM at all three follow-up points (1, 3,…

item.title: Peri-implantitis destroys faster with less bacteria: the vascular signature that explains it
item.description: In brief: In this large single-cell dataset (967,000+ cells), peri-implantitis tissues showed lower bacterial load and microbial diversity than…

item.title: Naringin curbs alveolar bone loss in rat periodontitis — via NLRP3 and the gut axis
item.description: In brief: In a rat ligature model, naringin reduced alveolar bone loss and suppressed NLRP3 inflammasome activation via the AMPK/Nrf2 pathway.

item.title: Allogeneic bone plug vs DBBM for GBR: comparable bone, faster turnover in pilot RCT
item.description: In brief: At 6 months, vital bone fraction did not differ significantly between ACBP and DBBM (38.8% vs 32.2%, P=0.105) in this pilot RCT.

item.title: One-piece vs. two-piece implants at 15+ years: better bone, but at what cost?
item.description: In brief: Overall implant survival reached 95% at 15-17 years, with two-piece implants showing higher survival (98.0%) than one-piece (91.8%) in this…
item.category: Clinical Implant Dentistry and Related Research

item.title: Silver-coated implants curb bone loss in peri-implantitis — but only in mice, so far
item.description: In brief: Ag⁺ ions on a hydroxyapatite/IP6 coating showed significant antibacterial activity against P. gingivalis in vitro.

item.title: Periodontitis and muscle loss: gingival activin A as a systemic mediator
item.description: In brief: In a mouse model, periodontitis reduced skeletal muscle mass through elevated circulating activin A, independent of food intake or fat mass…

item.title: Healthy implants, higher MMP-8: subclinical tissue stress before any clinical sign
item.description: In brief: In this matched-pair cohort, clinically healthy implants showed MMP-8 levels approximately 29% higher than adjacent homologous teeth (p<0.05).

item.title: Sinus lift in severe atrophy: a composite allograft + PRF protocol delivers histologically mature bone at six months
item.description: In brief: In this 60-biopsy single-arm cohort (residual height ≤4 mm), newly formed bone averaged 31.28% at six months — histologically mature with…

item.title: Ridge split vs. bone expansion: which technique costs more crestal bone at 12 months?
item.description: In brief: In this 72-site prospective study, conventional drilling produced the least crestal bone loss; ridge split the most, with bone expansion…

item.title: One nanocarrier, three targets: can DNA frameworks redefine adjunctive periodontitis therapy?
item.description: In brief: A tetrahedral DNA nanostructure (tFNA) co-delivers curcumin and defensin, showing antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and osteogenic activity in a…

item.title: Early peri-implantitis: mechanical debridement alone holds up — adjuncts show no significant edge
item.description: In brief: In this 6-month RCT (80 implants, 26 patients), all five nonsurgical protocols significantly improved probing depth, bleeding on probing, and…

item.title: Local statins in periodontal therapy: solid evidence for Step 2, limited for surgery
item.description: In brief: In Step 2 (non-surgical) therapy, locally delivered statins added ~1.4–2.3 mm PPD reduction over SRP alone across 13 RCTs.
